title: Fix Beads auto-start port 0 failure
description: After migrating Beads into bogorad.github.io, the database and imported issues work after an explicit 'bd dolt start', but normal commands after a stop still fail with 'dolt circuit breaker is open' and 'Port: 0'. Reproduction: (1) run 'bd dolt start', (2) confirm 'bd status' works, (3) run 'bd dolt stop --force', (4) run 'bd status' again. Expected: Beads auto-starts the Dolt server and status succeeds. Actual: Beads reports port 0 and fails fast. Notes: 'bd init -p bogorad-new --database bogorad_github_io --skip-agents --skip-hooks' succeeded, issue import succeeded, remote push succeeded, and 'bd dolt show' reports Host 127.0.0.1, Port 0, Mode per-project when the server is not manually running.
notes:
Rechecking current Beads/Dolt failure. Current evidence points to an invalid Dolt remote URL using git+ssh, not the old port 0 startup failure.
Removed invalid Dolt remote origin from bogorad_github_io. bd dolt pull now succeeds and skips because no remote is configured.
close_reason: Current Beads/Dolt failure was invalid remote configuration, not port 0 startup. Removed the bad origin remote; bd dolt pull now exits successfully with no remote configured.

title: Add GitHub Actions workflow for Hugo Pages deployment
description:
Scope:
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/ that builds the Hugo site from repository root and deploys it to GitHub Pages
- Use the standard Pages artifact/deploy pattern rather than committing generated output back into the repo
- Ensure the workflow runs on pushes to main and can also be run manually if useful
- Set required permissions for Pages deployment and define the github-pages environment if needed
- Ensure the workflow installs the correct Hugo version or uses a stable setup action

Checks:
- Workflow validates on push and/or in GitHub Actions UI
- Workflow builds the site from source rather than relying on committed public/
- Deployment artifact contains the generated static site
- The workflow does not depend on old Jekyll/Gemfile tooling

Out of scope:
- Do not switch repository Pages settings inside this task unless needed for testing
- Do not preserve generated public/ in git history
notes: Added .github/workflows/hugo.yml to build and deploy the root-level Hugo site through GitHub Pages artifacts. Latest successful deployment run used commit 4f3bf69. GitHub still emits a Node 20 deprecation warning from the current official Pages actions (configure-pages@v5, deploy-pages@v4, upload-pages-artifact@v4); the workflow is already on the current major versions, so this remaining warning is upstream action runtime behavior rather than repo-local site code.

title: Clone live repo and freeze current site into archive/legacy-site
description:
Scope:
- Clone bogorad/bogorad.github.io into /home/chuck/git/bogorad.github.io using gh repo clone
- Inspect current default branch, Pages settings, and working tree state in the cloned repo
- Create branch archive/legacy-site from the current live main branch before replacing anything
- Push archive/legacy-site so the frozen legacy version exists on GitHub, not only locally
- Do not edit archive/legacy-site after creation except for emergency recovery

Checks:
- Confirm repository remote points to bogorad/bogorad.github.io
- Confirm archive/legacy-site tip matches the pre-migration main commit
- Confirm branch is visible on GitHub after push
- Record the preserved commit SHA in issue notes for rollback reference

Out of scope:
- No replacement-site changes on main yet
- No Pages setting changes yet
notes: Preserved live main commit 7095433 on remote branch archive/legacy-site. Clone created at /home/chuck/git/bogorad.github.io and branch pushed successfully before any main-branch replacement work.

title: Remove legacy Jekyll files from main and keep only intentional replacements
description:
Scope:
- Audit the current bogorad.github.io main branch contents after the Hugo source is staged for migration
- Remove Jekyll-era files and directories from main that are no longer part of the new site, such as _config.yml, _layouts/, _includes/, _sass/, _data/, Gemfile, Gemfile.lock, legacy root HTML files, and other obsolete assets
- Evaluate whether any legacy file should be kept intentionally for the new site (for example favicon or README content) and document that choice explicitly
- Treat the old CNAME carefully: determine whether it is stale and should be dropped or intentionally preserved

Checks:
- Main branch contains only files required by the new Hugo site, deployment workflow, and intentional repo docs/metadata
- No dead Jekyll config remains on main by accident
- Any kept legacy file has a documented reason

Out of scope:
- Do not delete the preserved legacy branch
- Do not change Pages settings in this task

title: Map and move the Hugo site to repository root
description:
Scope:
- Treat the current Hugo site source as the migration input
- Move/adapt the new Hugo site so the live repository root becomes the Hugo project root
- Root-level target structure should include hugo.toml, content/, data/, layouts/, assets/, static/, and any root-level docs or metadata intentionally kept
- Update any repo-relative assumptions that only worked because the site previously lived under bogorad-site/
- Ensure local build commands run from repository root after the move

Checks:
- hugo --panicOnWarning --noBuildLock succeeds from repo root
- hugo server works from repo root
- No source file still depends on the old nested bogorad-site/ path layout
- Root-level structure is documented clearly for future edits

Out of scope:
- No GitHub Pages settings changes in this task
- No deletion of legacy Jekyll files unless required by the root migration work

title: Cut over GitHub Pages from legacy branch publishing to GitHub Actions
description:
Scope:
- Update GitHub Pages settings for bogorad/bogorad.github.io from the current legacy deploy-from-branch mode to GitHub Actions
- Verify the Pages source changes from branch root publishing to Actions-based deployment
- Ensure the first Actions-based deployment targets https://bogorad.github.io/
- Confirm HTTPS remains enforced and no unexpected custom-domain configuration is left active

Checks:
- Pages settings show GitHub Actions as the deployment source
- A deployment run completes successfully and GitHub reports the site as built/deployed
- https://bogorad.github.io/ serves the new Hugo site after cutover
- If a custom domain is not intended, no stale CNAME/domain state remains in effect

Out of scope:
- Do not preserve old branch-root deployment behavior
- Do not modify archive/legacy-site
notes: Switched GitHub Pages from legacy branch-root publishing to workflow mode via the Pages REST API. Verified build_type=workflow, source=main:/, html_url=https://bogorad.github.io/, and https_enforced=true. Main branch pushes cfb3262, fb699f2, and 4f3bf69 deployed successfully.

title: Verify production cutover and rollback readiness
description:
Scope:
- After deployment, verify the live site at https://bogorad.github.io/ on desktop and mobile
- Check that the homepage renders correctly, the main contact links work, the timezone interaction works on touch/mobile, and core assets load successfully
- Confirm the live repo structure matches the intended root-level Hugo source layout
- Confirm archive/legacy-site remains available as a rollback reference
- Document a simple rollback path using the preserved branch and/or revert commits if the cutover fails later

Checks:
- Live desktop render matches expected design closely
- Live mobile render and touch interactions behave as expected
- Major links/icons and generated assets resolve successfully
- archive/legacy-site remains unchanged and accessible
- Rollback instructions are written in issue notes or repo docs

Out of scope:
- No new feature work
- No design changes except break/fix discovered during verification
notes: Production verification passed. Desktop live site title, hero heading, subtitle, and contact order matched expectations. Mobile verification confirmed the timezone bubble appears on tap, shows 'Central European Time', and auto-hides after about 3 seconds. Rollback reference remains archive/legacy-site at 7095433a97b7958b44f8b73b9dab544e148bc7b2. Rollback path: restore main from archive/legacy-site (or revert the migration commits) and push main to trigger a Pages redeploy.

title: Migrate bogorad.github.io from legacy Jekyll setup to root-level Hugo with GitHub Actions
description:
Goal: replace the current live user-site repository bogorad/bogorad.github.io with the new Hugo-based site while preserving the existing public URL https://bogorad.github.io/ and keeping the old site available on a frozen branch named archive/legacy-site.

Target architecture:
- Keep repository name bogorad.github.io unchanged
- Preserve current main branch contents by branching archive/legacy-site from current live main before any destructive changes
- Replace main with the new Hugo site source at repository root (not nested in a subdirectory)
- Use GitHub Actions for Pages build and deploy instead of the current legacy branch-root Pages build
- Treat public/ as local/generated output only; do not deploy from committed public/ contents
- Add gitignore rules so generated Hugo output is not mistaken for deploy input
- Remove old Jekyll-era files from main unless explicitly kept for the new site

Constraints and decisions already made:
- Old site stays as branch archive/legacy-site, not as a renamed or archived repo
- New site should live at repo root, not under bogorad-site/
- GitHub Actions should build on GitHub from source and deploy the resulting artifact
- public/ should be gitignored in the live repo
- Keep the public URL unchanged at https://bogorad.github.io/
- Do not carry forward stale legacy data unless deliberately chosen (example: old CNAME looked stale during earlier inspection)

Definition of done:
- archive/legacy-site exists and preserves the current live site
- main contains only the new Hugo source/layout needed for the replacement site
- legacy Jekyll files are removed from main unless intentionally retained
- GitHub Pages is configured to deploy via GitHub Actions
- A successful Pages deployment publishes the new site at https://bogorad.github.io/
- Local testing instructions remain clear and public/ is ignored in git
- Post-cutover verification confirms desktop/mobile render, major links, and Pages settings
notes: Migration executed successfully in /home/chuck/git/bogorad.github.io. The old live site is preserved on archive/legacy-site, main now contains the root-level Hugo source, GitHub Pages deploys through Actions, and the live URL https://bogorad.github.io/ is serving the new site.

title: Fix missing Hugo scripts partial

description: Reproduce the broken Twitter/X icon on the homepage, fix the icon rendering, and change the hover text to X/Twitter without changing the rest of the contact rail design.

title: Remove leftover refactor artifacts
description: Delete files and data that are no longer part of the refactored Hugo structure, without touching unrelated user config. Verify references first, remove unused artifacts, rebuild Hugo, and confirm the working tree only contains intentional refactor changes.
notes: Removed the unused [location] block from content/_index.md, deleted the old static/images/bcn.png source, rebuilt Hugo, and confirmed public/images now contains only profile.jpg and bcn-mini.webp. Also updated REAMDE.md so it no longer mentions the removed PNG or unused content field.

title: Fix timezone icon not showing on page
description: The resized Barcelona timezone icon file is reachable directly, but the icon is not visibly rendering on the homepage. Reproduce against the running local page, identify whether the issue is stale HTML, template output, or CSS/computed style, and fix it without changing the design.
notes: Reproduced manually on http://127.0.0.1:1313 because the site has no browser test harness. Root cause: .contact-links

title: Split homepage into Hugo base and partials
description: Replace the monolithic home template with baseof, home, and partial templates while preserving DOM structure, CSS hooks, and visual output.

title: Move CSS to Hugo assets and verify parity
description: Move the stylesheet to Hugo assets with Hugo Pipes, eliminate inline presentation where safe, rebuild, and verify the design still matches on desktop and mobile.

title: Move homepage content into Hugo content and data
description: Move homepage copy and editable fields out of hugo.toml into content/_index.md and structured data files, preserving the existing rendered content and order.

title: Refactor Hugo site to use Hugo primitives
description: Analyze the existing bogorad-site and refactor it toward idiomatic Hugo structure while preserving the current visual design exactly. Replace monolithic config/template usage with human-editable Hugo primitives where they fit best, such as content files, data files, base templates, partials, and Hugo asset handling where safe. Avoid forcing primitives that add complexity without improving editability. Preserve rendered layout, class hooks, responsive behavior, and visual styling.
notes: Implementation is complete in the current branch/directory. Source refactor builds cleanly and the child subtasks are closed. Parent stays open pending user review and commit decision.

title: Ignore local Hugo build output and local-only artifacts
description:
Scope:
- Update the live repo .gitignore for the root-level Hugo layout
- Ignore /public/ so generated output is not mistaken for deploy input
- Ignore .hugo_build.lock and any other Hugo local build artifacts that should not be committed
- Keep the ignore rules minimal and focused on generated/local files only

Checks:
- git status remains clean after local Hugo builds except for intentional source changes
- public/ is clearly excluded from commits in the live repo
- Ignore rules do not hide real source files by mistake

Out of scope:
- Do not add broad ignore patterns unrelated to the website repo
- Do not remove intentional tracked assets
